---
title: "Population PK Portfolio: Theophylline"
subtitle: "A complete end-to-end pharmacokinetics workflow in R"
author: "Pramod BR"
date: today
---

## Overview

This portfolio demonstrates a complete Population Pharmacokinetics (PopPK) workflow using
the classic **Theophylline** dataset. It was built to showcase practical skills in clinical
pharmacology, DMPK, and pharmacometrics for roles in the pharmaceutical industry and CROs.

## Analysis Phases

| Phase | Report | Key Skills |
|-------|--------|-----------|
| 1 | [Exploratory Data Analysis](01_EDA_Report.html) | Data visualisation, PK study design |
| 2 | [Noncompartmental Analysis](02_NCA_Report.html) | PKNCA, NCA parameters, AUC, half-life |
| 3 | [Base PopPK Model](03_Base_Model_Report.html) | nlmixr2, 1-compartment model, FOCE |
| 4 | [IIV Model](04_IIV_Model_Report.html) | Between-subject variability, random effects |
| 5 | [Covariate Model](05_Covariate_Model_Report.html) | Body weight effects, model selection |
| 6 | [Model Diagnostics](06_Model_Diagnostics.html) | GOF plots, VPC, CWRES |
| 7 | [Clinical Simulation](07_Simulation_Report.html) | rxode2, dose individualisation |

## Dataset

The **Theoph** dataset (built-in to R) contains oral theophylline PK data from 12 subjects.
It is a benchmark dataset used in PopPK methodology papers and textbooks worldwide.

## Tools & Packages

- **nlmixr2** – Population PK/PD modeling
- **rxode2** – ODE-based simulation
- **PKNCA** – Noncompartmental analysis
- **ggplot2 / patchwork** – Publication-quality figures
- **Quarto** – Reproducible reports
- **renv** – Reproducible package environment

---

*Disclaimer: This project uses publicly available data for educational purposes only.*
